content.problemStatement:
You are given a set SSS, which is a subset of {1,2,‚Ä¶,N}\{1, 2, \ldots, N\}{1,2,‚Ä¶,N}.

Consider a permutation P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1‚Äã,P2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äã of the integers 111 through NNN. The cost of this permutation is the number of indices i>1i \gt 1i>1 such that PiP_iPi‚Äã is divisible by Pi‚àí1P_{i-1}Pi‚àí1‚Äã and PiPi‚àí1‚ààS\frac{P_i}{P_{i-1}} \in SPi‚àí1‚ÄãPi‚Äã‚Äã‚ààS.

For each kkk between 000 and N‚àí1N-1N‚àí1 (inclusive), find the number of permutations with cost kkk. Since these numbers may be large, compute them modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains a binary string TTT with length NNN. For each valid iii, the iii-th character of TTT is '1' if i‚ààSi \in Si‚ààS or '0' otherwise.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers. For each kkk (0‚â§k<N0 \le k \lt N0‚â§k<N), the k+1k+1k+1-th of these integers should be the number of permutations with cost kkk, modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
